paper.title_en: Nuclear structure investigation of the near-spherical nucleus <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Nb</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>95</mn></mmultiscripts></math>
paper.abstract_en: The excited states of <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Nb</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>95</mn></mmultiscripts></math> have been investigated using the heavy-ion fusion-evaporation reaction <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Se</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>82</mn></mmultiscripts></math>(<math><mrow><mmultiscripts><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>18</mn></mmultiscripts><mo>,</mo><mi>p</mi><mn>4</mn><mi>n</mi><mo>)</mo><mmultiscripts><mi>Nb</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>95</mn></mmultiscripts></mrow></math> at the beam energies of 82 and 88 MeV. Particle-<math><mrow><mi>γ</mi><mtext>-</mtext><mi>γ</mi></mrow></math> and <math><mrow><mi>γ</mi><mtext>-</mtext><mi>γ</mi></mrow></math> coincidence measurements using CsI and HPGe arrays are employed to gain selectivity to Nb isotopic products. The level scheme of <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Nb</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>95</mn></mmultiscripts></math> was extended up to <math><mrow><mi>E</mi><mo>≈</mo><mn>12</mn></mrow></math> MeV and <math><mrow><msup><mi>I</mi><mi>π</mi></msup><mo>=</mo><mrow><mo>(</mo><mn>53</mn><mo>/</mo><msup><mn>2</mn><mo>−</mo></msup><mo>)</mo></mrow></mrow></math> with 32 new <math><mi>γ</mi></math>-ray transitions. The suggested positive parity sequence of earlier publication is modified to negative parity; a previously unobserved new decay sequence has been assigned as positive parity. The coupling of the odd proton occupying the <math><msub><mi>g</mi><mrow><mn>9</mn><mo>/</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> orbital to the levels in the even-even <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Zr</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>94</mn></mmultiscripts></math> core can account for the low-lying excited states of <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Nb</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>95</mn></mmultiscripts></math>. The observed experimental states are compared with theoretical shell-model calculations.
